title: Final Project
overview: You have completed the full CS50 AI journey. This week you will apply everything to build a real project.

blocks.title: What You Have Learned
blocks.body: You have completed the full CS50 AI journey — from Search to Language. This week you will apply everything to build a real project.
bullets: Week 0: Search — BFS, DFS, A* — AI finding paths and solutions
Week 1: Knowledge & Logic — IF-THEN rules, inference, knowledge base
Week 2: Uncertainty — probability, Bayes Theorem, spam filters
Week 3: Optimization — Hill Climbing, Simulated Annealing, CSP
Week 4: Machine Learning — supervised, unsupervised, reinforcement
Week 5: Neural Networks — layers, weights, deep learning, CNNs
Week 6: NLP — tokenization, embeddings, LLMs, transformers
Week 7: Ethics — bias, privacy, hallucinations, accountability

leftPanel.label: Real Life Analogy
leftPanel.title: Cooking a meal
leftPanel.description: You have learned all the ingredients — now it is time to cook a full meal

centerPanel.label: Programming Concept
centerPanel.textContent: All 8 weeks of AI concepts → Applied in one real project

rightPanel.label: Code Example
rightPanel.code: # Your project will use at least one AI concept from this course

blocks.question: Is the final project a chance to apply what you have learned to a real problem?
blocks.answer: Yes, it combines the theories into an actual application.

blocks.title: Choose Your Project
blocks.body: Choose one of the 4 project options. Each uses a different AI concept from the course.
bullets: Option 1: AI Chatbot — uses NLP and prompt engineering to answer questions on a topic
Option 2: AI Story Generator — user gives characters and setting; AI generates a short story
Option 3: AI Resume Builder — user inputs details; AI formats and generates a professional resume
Option 4: Search Visualizer — a diagram or slide showing how BFS and DFS solve the same maze differently

leftPanel.label: Real Life Analogy
leftPanel.title: Final exam project
leftPanel.description: pick the one that interests you most

centerPanel.label: Programming Concept
centerPanel.textContent: Choose concept → Define input/output → Build → Reflect → Present

rightPanel.label: Code Example
rightPanel.code: # Chatbot: input = user question, output = AI answer

blocks.question: Should your project clearly show which AI concept from the course it uses?
blocks.answer: Yes, clearly identifying the AI mechanism is a key requirement.

blocks.title: Project Steps
blocks.body: Follow these steps to complete the project.
bullets: Step 1: Choose your project from the 4 options
Step 2: Identify which AI concept it uses (search, ML, NLP, etc.)
Step 3: Define input — what does the user provide?
Step 4: Define output — what does the AI produce?
Step 5: Identify one ethical consideration relevant to your project
Step 6: Write a short reflection — what surprised you about how AI actually works?

blocks.title: Practice Exercise
blocks.instructions: Write a project plan: project name, AI concept used, input, output, ethical consideration
blocks.hint: Fill in all the required project elements.

keyTakeaways.text: You have completed 8 weeks of AI concepts — Search, Logic, Probability, Optimization, ML, Neural Networks, NLP, Ethics
The final project applies your learning to a real AI-powered application
Every project must show which AI concept it uses
Define clear input and output for your project
Every project must include one ethical consideration
Reflection is as important as the code — think about what surprised you

whatYouLearned: What AI, ML, and Deep Learning are and how they differ
How AI searches for solutions using BFS, DFS, and A*
How AI uses logic, probability, and optimization
How Machine Learning and Neural Networks work
How NLP and LLMs understand and generate language
How to identify and address ethical issues in AI
